expectations.text: Names BOTH `Electrode().heading.attributes` (or `.heading`) AND `Electrode.parents()` as the inspection path — heading shows the carried columns, parents shows the FK declarations that contributed them
expectations.evidence: Names both: 'heading.attributes shows the flattened schema... parents() shows the declarations that contributed those columns.'

expectations.text: Identifies the link as a `-> [nullable] Probe.Electrode` declaration (secondary FK to a part table, nullable) — does not claim Probe is a primary parent of Electrode
expectations.evidence: States: 'the link to probes is -> [nullable] Probe.Electrode (file src/spyglass/common/common_ephys.py:78), and it sits below the --- divider' and 'there is no -> Probe declaration on Electrode'.

expectations.text: Explains nullable secondary FK semantics: the probe_* columns appear in Electrode's heading via FK inheritance, but Electrode rows are allowed to have NULL there (so a session without probe metadata still ingests)
expectations.evidence: Explains: 'Nullable. The [nullable] modifier means a row in Electrode is allowed to have NULL for those probe_* fields. That's deliberate: it lets a session whose NWB file lacks probe metadata still ingest into Electrode.'

expectations.text: Does not invent a primary `-> Probe` declaration on Electrode — the actual link is to the part table Probe.Electrode and is below the `---` divider
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'A common confusion: there is no -> Probe declaration on Electrode. The link is to the part table Probe.Electrode.'
